title: [Epic] Localize Client Inspector UI (i18n)
body:
## Goal
Bring the local Client UI (`inspector.html`) to full feature parity with the Server Admin Portal by implementing a Vanilla JS internationalization (i18n) engine.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Translation Dictionary
- Embed a JSON dictionary object at the top of the `<script>` block in `inspector.html` containing keys for supported languages: `en` (English), `es` (Spanish), `de` (German), `fr` (French), `pt` (Portuguese), `ja` (Japanese).

### 2. DOM Updates
- Tag all translatable HTML elements (labels, buttons, headers, table headers) with `data-i18n="key_name"` attributes.
- Update dynamic JavaScript rendering (e.g., connection status, tooltips) to use a `t('key_name')` helper function.

### 3. Translation Engine
- Implement `setLanguage(lang)` function that iterates over `[data-i18n]` elements and updates their `innerText`.
- Persist the user's language selection in `localStorage` so it survives restarts.
- Auto-detect the browser's default language using `navigator.language` on first load.

### 4. Language Selector UI
- Add a clean, minimal `<select>` dropdown next to the 'Maintenance' toggle in the sidebar header to allow users to switch languages on the fly.

## Open Questions
- Should we use the exact same dictionary keys as the Admin Portal where they overlap, or keep them perfectly isolated?
- Are there any specific regional dialects you want to prioritize (e.g., pt-BR vs pt-PT)?

issues.title: Build i18n Translation Dictionary and Engine for Client UI
issues.body: Add the dictionary object, the `setLanguage` engine, and `localStorage` persistence logic to `inspector.html`.

issues.title: Tag HTML Elements and Update Dynamic Rendering
issues.body: Add `data-i18n` attributes to all static DOM elements and update dynamic JS templates to use the translation helper.

issues.title: Implement Language Selector Dropdown
issues.body: Add a language switcher dropdown to the sidebar header to allow users to dynamically change their UI language.
